questions.question: On which platforms can you currently use Notion Mail?

options.text: Mac, Web, and iOS (version 17 or later)
options.explanation: The article lists these three platforms as the currently supported ones.

options.text: Windows, Android, and Web
options.explanation: Windows support is announced for the future and Android isn’t mentioned.

options.text: Linux, macOS (Intel only), and iPadOS
options.explanation: Linux isn’t supported, and macOS support includes both Apple Silicon and Intel.

options.text: ChromeOS, Web, and Android
options.explanation: ChromeOS and Android are not listed as available platforms.

questions.question: How do you open Notion Mail from within the Notion app?

options.text: Click the ✉️ icon at the bottom of the sidebar
options.explanation: The article states you can access Notion Mail by selecting the mail icon at the bottom of the sidebar.

options.text: Press Ctrl + M on your keyboard
options.explanation: No keyboard shortcut is mentioned for opening Notion Mail.

options.text: Open the Settings menu and choose ‘Mail’
options.explanation: Mail is accessed via the sidebar icon, not through Settings.

options.text: Navigate to the ‘Apps’ section and click ‘Notion Mail’
options.explanation: There is no separate ‘Apps’ section; the sidebar icon is the entry point.

questions.question: What two things are required to sign up for Notion Mail?

options.text: A Notion account and a Gmail (or Google Workspace) address
options.explanation: The article explicitly lists a Notion account plus a Gmail/Workspace address as prerequisites.

options.text: A paid Notion subscription and an Outlook address
options.explanation: A paid subscription isn’t required and Outlook isn’t supported yet.

options.text: Two-factor authentication enabled and a corporate email address
options.explanation: These are not mentioned as sign‑up requirements.

options.text: A mobile device running iOS 16 and a Google Drive account
options.explanation: The OS version and Google Drive are irrelevant to sign‑up.

questions.question: If you delete a single email inside a Gmail thread, what happens in Notion Mail?

options.text: The email is deleted in Notion Mail as well
options.explanation: Only whole threads are synced for deletions; single‑email deletions are not reflected.

options.text: The email remains in Notion Mail because deletions sync at the thread level
options.explanation: The article explains that deletions sync at the thread level, so a single‑email deletion won’t appear in Notion Mail.

options.text: Notion Mail shows the email as ‘archived’ instead of deleted
options.explanation: Archiving isn’t mentioned; the behavior is simply that the email stays.

options.text: The email is moved to the Spam folder in Notion Mail
options.explanation: Spam handling is separate and unrelated to deletions.

questions.question: Which of the following Gmail elements are NOT synchronized to Notion Mail?

options.text: Labels and contacts
options.explanation: Labels and contacts are synced; they are listed as features that do sync.

options.text: Blocked senders, snoozed emails, scheduled emails, drafts, and spam (initial sync)
options.explanation: The article lists these specific items as not being synced from Gmail to Notion Mail.

options.text: Inbox view and read‑status counts
options.explanation: Read‑status counts may vary slightly but are generally synced; they’re not listed as unsynced.

options.text: Promotions category and custom filters
options.explanation: Categories like promotions are part of the Gmail features that do sync.

questions.question: How do you add a second Gmail account to an existing Notion Mail account?

options.text: Open the dropdown next to your name and choose “Add another account”
options.explanation: The article describes exactly this process for adding another Gmail account.

options.text: Create a new Notion workspace for the second email address
options.explanation: A separate workspace isn’t required; you add the account within the same Notion Mail account.

options.text: Contact Notion support to link the additional address
options.explanation: No support request is needed; the UI provides the option.

options.text: Install the Notion Mail desktop app on a different computer
options.explanation: Installing on another device doesn’t add a new Gmail account to the same Notion Mail profile.

questions.question: Where must you go to edit your email signature when using Notion Mail?

options.text: In the Notion Mail settings panel
options.explanation: Signature editing isn’t handled inside Notion Mail.

options.text: Directly in Gmail’s settings
options.explanation: The article notes that signature changes must be made in Gmail.

options.text: In the Notion AI assistant
options.explanation: Notion AI isn’t used for signature management.

options.text: Through the Windows Mail app
options.explanation: Windows Mail isn’t part of the Notion Mail workflow.

questions.question: If you rename a label in Gmail after the initial sync, what will happen in Notion Mail?

options.text: The new label name automatically updates in Notion Mail
options.explanation: Renaming in Gmail after the initial sync does NOT propagate to Notion Mail.

options.text: Notion Mail keeps the old label name; you must rename it in Notion Mail to sync back to Gmail
options.explanation: The article explains that changes made in Gmail after the first sync won’t reflect; you can rename in Notion Mail and it will sync to Gmail.

options.text: Both Gmail and Notion Mail display both the old and new label names
options.explanation: Only one label name is shown; duplication does not occur.

options.text: The label is deleted in Notion Mail and recreated with the new name
options.explanation: Deletion and recreation isn’t the described behavior.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the limitation of using Gmail aliases with Notion Mail?

options.text: Each alias can be used to create a separate Notion Mail account
options.explanation: The article explicitly says aliases cannot be used to sign up for additional Notion Mail accounts.

options.text: Only one email address (including aliases) can be associated with a single Notion Mail account
options.explanation: While only one primary email address is allowed per account, aliases can be used within that same account.

options.text: You can use any number of Gmail aliases within one Notion Mail account, but each alias requires its own Notion Mail account to function independently
options.explanation: Aliases work inside the same account; they don’t need separate Notion Mail accounts.

options.text: Aliases can be used inside a single Notion Mail account, but they cannot be used to create additional Notion Mail accounts
options.explanation: The article states that aliases are usable within a single account, yet they cannot serve to sign up for more accounts.

questions.question: What is the behavior of spam emails during the initial sync between Gmail and Notion Mail?

options.text: All spam emails are imported into Notion Mail during the first sync
options.explanation: Spam is explicitly excluded from the initial sync.

options.text: Spam emails are not imported initially, but after the first sync, marking an email as spam in either service updates the other
options.explanation: The article notes that spam isn’t synced on the first run, yet subsequent spam actions sync both ways.

options.text: Spam emails are imported but automatically deleted after 24 hours
options.explanation: There is no mention of automatic deletion; spam simply isn’t part of the initial import.

options.text: Only spam from the promotions category is synced
options.explanation: Spam handling is not limited to a specific category; it’s excluded initially.
